title: Authors and Citation
text: Pedro Cardoso. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cardoso P (2026). cateval: Validation Conditional Average Treatment Effects. R package version 1.5.0, https://PM-Cardoso.github.io/cateval/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {cateval: Validation of Conditional Average Treatment Effects},   author = {Pedro Cardoso},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.5.0},   url = {https://PM-Cardoso.github.io/cateval/}, }

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: R package validating Conditional Average Treatment Effects (CATE)

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: cateval provides comprehensive suite functions validate analyze conditional average treatment effects (CATE) causal inference studies. Whether ’re comparing two treatments analyzing multiple treatment scenarios, package offers tools : Estimating validating heterogeneous treatment effects Calibrating predictions subgroup analysis Identifying optimal treatments individual patients populations Assessing treatment benefit across treatment arms Visualizing treatment comparisons outcomes package designed pairwise comparisons (two treatments) multi-arm studies (multiple treatments), making flexible various study designs.

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: match_benefit_pairs() — Match concordant patients (received recommended drug) discordant patients, directions, returning concordant, discordant combined matched populations inspection benefit_deciles() — Mean observed benefit group predicted benefit (calibration-plot data) benefit_by_drug() — , split recommended drug class benefit_overall() — Mean observed benefit following model benefit_calibration() — Calibration---large calibration slope predicted benefit summary reports analytical confidence intervals default, optional pair-level bootstrap intervals. Plotting left user (see worked example) calibration plot can built whatever detail analysis calls — package produces underlying group pair-level numbers.

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: unified_validation() — Predicted vs observed treatment effects across drug pairs calibration_hte() — underlying single-pair calibration engine

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: get_best_drugs() — Identify recommended drug (near-best set) patient get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs() — Rank select treatments meeting tolerance threshold optimal_drug_comparison_plot() — Visualise distribution recommended drug combinations

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: Continuous outcomes Matching-based causal inference (via MatchIt integration) Publication-ready visualizations ggplot2 Tidy data workflows dplyr tidyr

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: Full API reference examples: https://PM-Cardoso.github.io/cateval/

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: Install latest development version GitHub:
code: if (!require("devtools")) {   install.packages("devtools") } devtools::install_github("PM-Cardoso/cateval", dependencies = TRUE, build_vignettes = FALSE)

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
code: library(cateval)  drugs <- c("SGLT2", "GLP1", "DPP4", "SU", "TZD")  # Pairwise heterogeneous treatment effect calibration across all drug pairs pairwise_results <- unified_validation(   data            = your_data,   drug_var        = "drugclass",   drugs           = drugs,   prediction_vars = paste0("pred.", drugs),   outcome_var     = "posthba1cfinal",   cal_groups      = c(3, 5, 10),   adjustment_var  = c("agetx", "sex", "prehba1c") )  # Overall (concordant vs discordant) benefit calibration # 1. match, keeping the matched populations for inspection matched <- match_benefit_pairs(   data         = your_data,   drug_var     = "drugclass",   outcome_var  = "posthba1cfinal",   pred_cols    = paste0("pred.", drugs),   matching_var = c("agetx", "sex", "prehba1c"),   match.exact  = "sex",   caliper      = c(prehba1c = 0.4),   seed         = 19840503 )  # 2. summarise (set bootstrap = TRUE for pair-level bootstrap intervals) deciles <- benefit_deciles(matched$combined, cal_groups = 10) overall <- benefit_overall(matched$combined) slope   <- benefit_calibration(matched$combined)  # 3. plot the calibration yourself: group means + identity line + a LOESS # curve fitted on the group (decile) point estimates library(ggplot2) ggplot(deciles, aes(x = pred.benefit, y = obs.benefit)) +   geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, colour = "red", linetype = "dashed") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, span = 1, colour = "blue") +   geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.ci, ymax = upper.ci), width = 0.2) +   geom_point(size = 2.5)  # Identify the predicted-optimal treatment(s) for each patient best_drugs <- get_best_drugs(   data         = your_data,   rank         = 1,   column_names = paste0("pred.", drugs),   final_var_name = "pred." )  # Visualise the distribution of predicted-optimal drug combinations optimal_drug_comparison_plot(   data   = best_drugs$pred.within_3_of_best_drug_name,   groups = list("1-drug" = 1, "2-drug" = 2, "3+-drug" = 3:5) )

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: use cateval research, please cite:
code: @software{cardoso2024cateval,   author = {Cardoso, Pedro},   title = {cateval: Validation of Conditional Average Treatment Effects},   year = {2024},   url = {https://github.com/PM-Cardoso/cateval} }

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: GPL (≥ 3). See LICENSE.md details.

title: cateval — Validating Predicted Treatment Benefit
text: Found bug suggestions? Please open issue GitHub Issues.

what: Overall-Benefit Calibration Split by Drug Class — benefit_by_drug
title: Overall-Benefit Calibration Split by Drug Class — benefit_by_drug
text: Runs benefit_deciles separately within drug class, matched pair assigned drug class received index (matched) patient. shows whether model's benefit predictions well calibrated within group pairs anchored drug class.

title: Overall-Benefit Calibration Split by Drug Class — benefit_by_drug
code: benefit_by_drug(   pairs,   drugs = NULL,   cal_groups = 10,   bootstrap = FALSE,   n_boot = 1000,   boot_seed = NULL,   conf = 0.95 )

title: Overall-Benefit Calibration Split by Drug Class — benefit_by_drug
text: pairs matched-pairs data frame match_benefit_pairs containing index_drug, pred.benefit obs.benefit. drugs Optional character vector drug classes report, desired order. Supply full set (rather relying drugs happen appear pairs) output plot show consistent set drug classes even drug class never anchors matched pair. Drug classes matched pairs summarised dropped warning; returned index_drug column factor whose levels follow drugs. NULL (default), drug classes present pairs used. cal_groups Integer. Number groups per drug class (e.g. 10). Passed benefit_deciles. bootstrap Logical. Use pair-level bootstrap confidence intervals. n_boot Integer. Number bootstrap replicates. boot_seed Optional integer. Seed reproducibility. conf Numeric. Confidence level (default 0.95).

title: Overall-Benefit Calibration Split by Drug Class — benefit_by_drug
text: data frame stacking benefit_deciles output drug class, added index_drug column identifying drug class anchors group. Suitable faceting.

title: Overall-Benefit Calibration Split by Drug Class — benefit_by_drug
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ benefit_by_drug(matched$combined, cal_groups = 10) } # }

what: Calibration-in-the-Large and Calibration Slope of Predicted Benefit — benefit_calibration
title: Calibration-in-the-Large and Calibration Slope of Predicted Benefit — benefit_calibration
text: Regresses observed benefit predicted benefit across matched pairs returns two calibration summaries: calibration---large (intercept, .e. average observed benefit predicted benefit zero) calibration slope (much observed benefit changes per unit predicted benefit; 1 indicates perfect calibration).

title: Calibration-in-the-Large and Calibration Slope of Predicted Benefit — benefit_calibration
code: benefit_calibration(   pairs,   bootstrap = FALSE,   n_boot = 1000,   boot_seed = NULL,   conf = 0.95 )

title: Calibration-in-the-Large and Calibration Slope of Predicted Benefit — benefit_calibration
text: pairs matched-pairs data frame match_benefit_pairs containing pred.benefit obs.benefit. bootstrap Logical. FALSE (default) intervals analytical regression confidence intervals. TRUE percentile bootstrap intervals resample matched pairs independently (one row = one pair). relax regression's homoscedastic-error assumption, account dependence reusing control patient across pairs (matching replacement). n_boot Integer. Number bootstrap replicates. boot_seed Optional integer. Seed reproducibility. conf Numeric. Confidence level (default 0.95).

title: Calibration-in-the-Large and Calibration Slope of Predicted Benefit — benefit_calibration
text: data frame two rows (calibration_in_the_large calibration_slope) columns estimate, lower.ci upper.ci.

title: Calibration-in-the-Large and Calibration Slope of Predicted Benefit — benefit_calibration
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ benefit_calibration(matched$combined) benefit_calibration(matched$combined, bootstrap = TRUE, boot_seed = 42) } # }

what: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
title: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
text: Divides matched pairs equal-sized groups (e.g. deciles) predicted benefit returns mean observed benefit group, confidence interval. data behind overall-benefit calibration plot: mean observed benefit group compared mean predicted benefit line identity.

title: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
code: benefit_deciles(   pairs,   cal_groups = 10,   bootstrap = FALSE,   n_boot = 1000,   boot_seed = NULL,   conf = 0.95 )

title: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
text: pairs matched-pairs data frame match_benefit_pairs (concordant, discordant combined element), containing pred.benefit obs.benefit. cal_groups Integer. Number groups split predicted benefit (e.g. 10 deciles). bootstrap Logical. FALSE (default) confidence interval analytical t-interval group mean. TRUE interval percentile bootstrap resamples matched pairs. pairs resampled independently (one row = one pair), group mean interval usually close analytical t-interval; widen dependence created control patient reused across several pairs (matching replacement). differs analytical interval mainly group pairs skewed distribution observed benefit. n_boot Integer. Number bootstrap replicates (used bootstrap = TRUE). boot_seed Optional integer. Seed set bootstrapping reproducibility. conf Numeric. Confidence level interval (default 0.95).

title: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
text: data frame one row per group: group Group index, ordered increasing predicted benefit. pred.benefit Mean predicted benefit group. obs.benefit Mean observed benefit group. n Number matched pairs group. lower.ci, upper.ci Confidence-interval bounds mean observed benefit.

title: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
text: group boundaries fixed predicted benefit pairs bootstrapping, bootstrap replicate re-estimates mean observed benefit group patients rather shifting boundaries replicates.

title: Group Matched Pairs by Predicted Benefit and Summarise Observed Benefit — benefit_deciles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ benefit_deciles(matched$combined, cal_groups = 10) benefit_deciles(matched$combined, cal_groups = 10, bootstrap = TRUE, boot_seed = 42) } # }

what: Mean Observed Benefit of Following the Model — benefit_overall
title: Mean Observed Benefit of Following the Model — benefit_overall
text: Summarises set matched pairs single number: average observed benefit concordance (receiving predicted-best drug), confidence interval. headline "much following model pay " estimate, typically reported pooled (combined) matched set.

title: Mean Observed Benefit of Following the Model — benefit_overall
code: benefit_overall(   pairs,   bootstrap = FALSE,   n_boot = 1000,   boot_seed = NULL,   conf = 0.95 )

title: Mean Observed Benefit of Following the Model — benefit_overall
text: pairs matched-pairs data frame match_benefit_pairs containing obs.benefit. bootstrap Logical. FALSE (default) interval analytical t-interval mean. TRUE percentile bootstrap resamples matched pairs independently (one row = one pair). mean many pairs usually close analytical interval; account dependence reusing control patient across pairs (matching replacement). n_boot Integer. Number bootstrap replicates. boot_seed Optional integer. Seed reproducibility. conf Numeric. Confidence level (default 0.95).

title: Mean Observed Benefit of Following the Model — benefit_overall
text: one-row data frame observed (mean observed benefit), lower.ci, upper.ci n (number pairs).

title: Mean Observed Benefit of Following the Model — benefit_overall
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ benefit_overall(matched$combined) benefit_overall(matched$combined, bootstrap = TRUE, boot_seed = 42) } # }

what: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
title: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
text: Estimates heterogeneous treatment effects two drugs stratifying patients calibration groups based predicted benefit scores. Optionally performs covariate matching estimating treatment effects.

title: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
code: calibration_hte(   data,   drug_var,   drugs,   benefit_var,   outcome_var,   cal_groups,   matching = FALSE,   adjustment_var = NULL,   matching_var = adjustment_var,   match.exact = NULL,   match.antiexact = NULL )

title: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
text: data data frame containing observed outcomes, treatment assignments, predicted benefits. drug_var Character string. Column name treatment assignment variable (e.g., drug). drugs Character vector length 2. Names two drugs compare. benefit_var Character string. Column name containing predicted benefit scores. outcome_var Character string. Column name outcome variable (e.g., clinical measurement). cal_groups Numeric numeric vector. Number(s) calibration groups (e.g., quantiles) divide data based predicted benefit. matching Logical. Whether perform covariate matching using MatchIt package estimating treatment effects. adjustment_var Optional character vector. Names covariates include adjustment variables regression model. matching_var Optional character vector. Covariates use matching. Defaults adjustment_var specified. match.exact Optional character vector. Variables exact matching. Matching best predicted drug automatically added. match.antiexact Optional character vector. Variables anti-exact matching. drug_var automatically added.

title: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
text: data frame row corresponds calibration group within cal_groups setting. Columns include: mean mean predicted benefit score patients calibration group. coef Estimated average treatment effect (regression coefficient) comparing two drugs, adjusted covariates specified. coef_low Lower bound 95% confidence interval treatment effect. coef_high Upper bound 95% confidence interval treatment effect. n_groups Number calibration groups (.e., value cal_groups) used create stratification. drug1 Name first drug comparison (drugs[1]). n_drug1 Number patients receiving drug1 within calibration group. drug2 Name second drug comparison (drugs[2]). n_drug2 Number patients receiving drug2 within calibration group.

title: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
text: multiple values provided cal_groups, function repeat process value return combined result.

title: Estimate Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Across Calibration Groups — calibration_hte
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage without matching or adjustment result <- calibration_hte(   data = test_data,   drug_var = "drugclass",   drugs = c("SGLT2", "DPP4"),   benefit_var = "benefit_score",   outcome_var = "posthba1cfinal",   cal_groups = 5 )  # With adjustment result_adj <- calibration_hte(   data = test_data,   drug_var = "drugclass",   drugs = c("SGLT2", "DPP4"),   benefit_var = "benefit_score",   outcome_var = "posthba1cfinal",   cal_groups = 3,   adjustment_var = c("age", "bmi", "sex") )  # With matching and exact matching result_match <- calibration_hte(   data = test_data,   drug_var = "drugclass",   drugs = c("SGLT2", "DPP4"),   benefit_var = "benefit_score",   outcome_var = "posthba1cfinal",   cal_groups = 4,   matching = TRUE,   adjustment_var = c("age", "bmi", "sex"),   match.exact = "sex" ) } # }

what: cateval: Validation of Conditional Average Treatment Effects — cateval-package
title: cateval: Validation of Conditional Average Treatment Effects — cateval-package
text: Tools validating calibration predicted treatment benefit treatment-selection models continuous outcome. package covers two calibration analyses used validation procedure: overall benefit, based matched concordant discordant patients, pairwise drug-class effects. Benefit summarised group (calibration plot), mean benefit following model, calibration---large calibration slope, analytical optional pair-level bootstrap confidence intervals.

title: cateval: Validation of Conditional Average Treatment Effects — cateval-package
text: Maintainer: Pedro Cardoso p.cardoso@exeter.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Assign Best Drugs Based on Prediction Rankings or Tolerance — get_best_drugs
title: Assign Best Drugs Based on Prediction Rankings or Tolerance — get_best_drugs
text: Applies get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs across rows dataset determine best (nearly best) drugs based model predictions. Adds result new columns data.

title: Assign Best Drugs Based on Prediction Rankings or Tolerance — get_best_drugs
code: get_best_drugs(   data,   rank = 1,   column_names = NULL,   final_var_name = "",   tolerance = NULL )

title: Assign Best Drugs Based on Prediction Rankings or Tolerance — get_best_drugs
text: data data frame containing predicted values specified columns. rank Integer. Rank prediction select (e.g., 1 = best, 2 = second-best). Ignored tolerance set. column_names Character vector. Names columns containing predicted values. final_var_name Character string. Base name output columns. tolerance Optional numeric. set, returns drugs within tolerance best.

title: Assign Best Drugs Based on Prediction Rankings or Tolerance — get_best_drugs
text: modified version original data frame two new columns: _drug_value Predicted value(s) selected drug(s). _drug_name Name(s) selected drug(s).

title: Assign Best Drugs Based on Prediction Rankings or Tolerance — get_best_drugs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ preds <- data.frame(pred_A = c(0.1, 0.3), pred_B = c(0.2, 0.2), pred_C = c(0.3, 0.1)) get_best_drugs(preds, rank = 1, column_names = names(preds), final_var_name = "best") get_best_drugs(preds, tolerance = 0.05, column_names = names(preds), final_var_name = "tolerant") } # }

what: Get Ranked or Tolerance-Based Drug Recommendation — get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs
title: Get Ranked or Tolerance-Based Drug Recommendation — get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs
text: function identifies drug(s) best (lowest) predicted outcome set model predictions. can return nth-best drug drugs within specified tolerance best drug.

title: Get Ranked or Tolerance-Based Drug Recommendation — get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs
code: get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs(   row,   rank = 1,   column_names = NULL,   prediction_column = NULL,   tolerance = NULL )

title: Get Ranked or Tolerance-Based Drug Recommendation — get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs
text: row named vector one-row data frame predictions single patient. rank Integer. Rank drug return (e.g., 1 = best, 2 = second-best). Ignored tolerance provided. column_names Character vector. Names columns containing predicted values. prediction_column Character string. Common prefix used prediction column names (e.g., "pred_"). tolerance Optional numeric. provided, return drugs within margin best predicted value.

title: Get Ranked or Tolerance-Based Drug Recommendation — get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs
text: character vector two named elements: value Comma-separated string predicted values (single value using rank). name Comma-separated string corresponding drug names (single name using rank).

title: Get Ranked or Tolerance-Based Drug Recommendation — get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ row <- c(pred_A = 0.2, pred_B = 0.5, pred_C = 0.21) get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs(   row,   rank = 1, column_names = names(row), prediction_column = "pred_" ) get_ranked_or_tolerant_drugs(   row,   tolerance = 0.05, column_names = names(row), prediction_column = "pred_" ) } # }

what: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
title: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
text: Builds matched patient pairs used validate treatment-selection model's predicted overall benefit. patient labelled concordant (received predicted-best drug) discordant (received different drug), concordant patients matched clinically similar discordant patients nearest-neighbour Mahalanobis distance, exactly predicted-best drug forced differ drug actually received.

title: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
code: match_benefit_pairs(   data,   drug_var,   outcome_var,   pred_cols,   matching_var,   match.exact = NULL,   match.antiexact = NULL,   caliper = NULL,   conc_tolerance = NULL,   symmetric = TRUE,   seed = NULL )

title: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
text: data data frame one row per patient, containing received drug, observed outcome, predicted outcome drug matching covariates. drug_var Character. Name column holding drug actually received. outcome_var Character. Name observed outcome column (e.g. 12-month HbA1c). Benefits computed absolute scale. pred_cols Character vector. Names predicted-outcome columns, one per drug, sharing common prefix (e.g. c("pred.SGLT2", "pred.GLP1")). matching_var Character vector. Covariates used Mahalanobis distance matching. match.exact Optional character vector. Additional variables match exactly. predicted-best drug always included. match.antiexact Optional character vector. Additional variables matched pair must differ. received-drug variable always included. caliper Optional named numeric vector passed MatchIt::matchit (e.g. c(prehba1c = 0.4)); interpreted standard-deviation units. conc_tolerance Optional numeric. supplied, patient concordant received drug within tolerance best predicted outcome, discordant match must differ every drug set. symmetric Logical. TRUE (default), match directions return pooled, de-duplicated set combined. FALSE, concordant--treated direction run. seed Optional integer. Seed set matching call result reproducible.

title: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
text: object class cateval_benefit_match: list elements concordant Matched pairs, concordant patients treated. discordant Matched pairs, discordant patients treated (NULL symmetric = FALSE). combined concordant discordant sets pooled, duplicate pairs removed. matchit list underlying matchit objects ($concordant $discordant) balance / love plots. pairs data frame one row per matched pair, including concordant_drug, discordant_drug, index_drug (drug received index/treated patient pair), pred.benefit, obs.benefit retained matching covariates.

title: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
text: Matching run directions default (symmetric = TRUE): concordant patients treated group roles reversed. returns three matched populations can inspected summarised separately: concordant - concordant patients matched discordant controls; discordant - discordant patients matched concordant controls; combined   - two sets pooled, pairs appear directions counted . predicted observed benefits computed scale outcome_var (.e. absolute outcomes): pair, pred.benefit concordant patient's predicted outcome discordant drug minus predicted outcome (recommended) drug, obs.benefit observed outcome discordant patient minus concordant patient. positive model's recommended drug expected , , give better outcome. matched pairs frames input benefit_deciles, benefit_overall, benefit_calibration benefit_by_drug. Limitation (confidence intervals matching replacement). Matching done replacement, single control patient can appear several matched pairs, makes pairs share control independent. confidence intervals summary functions – analytical interval optional pair-level bootstrap – treat matched pairs independent, widen reflect dependence. therefore likely somewhat narrow (anti-conservative) control reuse substantial. Valid variance estimation matching replacement known hard problem; intervals read approximate, limitation stated reported. extent reuse can inspected directly, e.g. table(table(c(pairs$cateval_row_id_conc, pairs$cateval_row_id_disc))).

title: Match Concordant and Discordant Patients for Overall-Benefit Calibration — match_benefit_pairs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ matched <- match_benefit_pairs(   data = analysis_dataset,   drug_var = "drugclass",   outcome_var = "posthba1cfinal",   pred_cols = paste0("pred.", c("SGLT2", "GLP1", "DPP4", "SU", "TZD")),   matching_var = c("agetx", "prebmi", "prehba1c", "sex"),   match.exact = c("sex"),   caliper = c(prehba1c = 0.4),   seed = 19840503 )  # inspect balance plot(summary(matched$matchit$concordant)) # summarise the pooled set benefit_deciles(matched$combined, cal_groups = 10) } # }

title: Plotting optimal drug combinations — optimal_drug_comparison_plot
text: function generates side--side bar plot visualizing: proportion individuals 1 5 optimal drugs identified. frequent drug combinations category (e.g., 1-drug, 2-drug).

title: Plotting optimal drug combinations — optimal_drug_comparison_plot
code: optimal_drug_comparison_plot(data, groups, plot = TRUE)

title: Plotting optimal drug combinations — optimal_drug_comparison_plot
text: data character vector element comma-separated string drugs considered optimal individual. groups named list name represents category (e.g., "1-drug combination") value vector drug counts (e.g., 1, 2, 4:5). plot Logical. TRUE (default), returns combined patchwork plot. FALSE, returns list summary data frames.

title: Plotting optimal drug combinations — optimal_drug_comparison_plot
text: patchwork object combining two ggplot2 bar plots side--side.

title: Plotting optimal drug combinations — optimal_drug_comparison_plot
code: groups <- list(   "1-drug combination" = 1,   "2-drug combinations" = 2,   "3-drug combinations" = 3,   "3/4/5-drug combinations" = 4:5 ) optimal_drug_comparison_plot(c("SGLT2,DPP4,GLP1", "GLP1", "TZD,DPP4"), groups)

what: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
title: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
text: function computes heterogeneous treatment effect calibration curves pairwise comparisons specified set drugs. pair, calculates predicted benefit (difference predicted outcomes), partitions patients calibration groups based predicted benefit quantiles, estimates treatment effects within group.

title: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
code: unified_validation(   data,   drug_var,   drugs,   prediction_vars,   outcome_var,   cal_groups,   matching = FALSE,   adjustment_var = NULL,   matching_var = adjustment_var,   match.exact = NULL,   match.antiexact = NULL )

title: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
text: data data.frame containing observed outcomes, treatment assignments, predicted outcomes drug. drug_var Character string specifying column name treatment variable (e.g., assigned drug). drugs Character vector two drug names compared pairwise. prediction_vars Character vector column names corresponding predicted outcomes drug. Must length order drugs. outcome_var Character string specifying column name observed outcome variable. cal_groups Numeric scalar vector specifying number(s) calibration groups (quantiles) partition population subgroup analysis. matching Logical; TRUE, performs covariate matching using MatchIt package estimating effects. adjustment_var Optional character vector covariate column names adjust regression models. matching_var Optional character vector specifying variables match . Defaults adjustment_var NULL. match.exact Optional character vector variables exact matching. best predicted drug always included. match.antiexact Optional character vector variables anti-exact matching. treatment variable always included.

title: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
text: data.frame one row per calibration group drug pair combination, including: cal_groups Number calibration groups used. grouping Calibration group identifier (e.g., 1 cal_groups). mean Mean predicted benefit calibration group. coef Estimated treatment effect (regression coefficient) within group. coef_low Lower bound 95% confidence interval estimate. coef_high Upper bound 95% confidence interval. drug1 Name first drug pair. n_drug1 Number patients group receiving first drug. drug2 Name second drug pair. n_drug2 Number patients group receiving second drug.

title: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
text: Internally, function calls calibration_hte() drug pair obtain subgroup treatment effect estimates confidence intervals.

title: Perform Pairwise Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Calibration Across Multiple Drugs — unified_validation
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example usage comparing 3 drugs with predicted outcomes results <- unified_validation(   data = mydata,   drug_var = "treatment",   drugs = c("DrugA", "DrugB", "DrugC"),   prediction_vars = c("pred_drugA", "pred_drugB", "pred_drugC"),   outcome_var = "outcome",   cal_groups = c(3, 5),        # test 3 and 5 calibration groups   adjustment_var = c("age", "sex"),   matching = TRUE ) head(results) } # }

title: cateval 1.5.0
text: match_benefit_pairs() replaces compute_overall_benefit() entry point. labels patient concordant discordant, runs symmetric 1:1 matching (concordant--treated discordant--treated), returns concordant, discordant de-duplicated combined matched populations together underlying matchit objects. New summary functions take matched-pairs frame return calibration numbers: benefit_deciles() (mean observed vs predicted benefit group), benefit_by_drug() (, split index/treated patient’s drug), benefit_overall() (mean observed benefit following model) benefit_calibration() (calibration---large calibration slope). summary offers analytical confidence intervals default optional pair-level percentile bootstrap (bootstrap = TRUE). Note bootstrap resamples matched pairs independent, account dependence introduced matching replacement. Predicted benefit anchored index (treated) patient pair, observed benefit computed absolute outcome scale.

title: cateval 1.5.0
text: compute_overall_benefit(), compute_overall_benefit_performance() plot_benefit_calibration() removed. Plotting left caller; package returns underlying numbers .

title: cateval 1.5.0
text: Added test suite continuous integration (R-CMD-check).
